summary: The standard root canal technique faces issues such as inaccurate shaping and incomplete cleaning of small or complex anatomy; it lacks precise control compared to modern tools and imaging technologies; concerns about effective removal of side branches persist despite using materials like Gutta-Percha, making thorough treatment challenging without additional advanced equipment. Agent1 summarized that although the standardized method has drawbacks including imprecise results from handwork alone versus newer tech-based approaches, along with difficulties achieving complete cleanliness especially within intricate structures where minor errors could lead to infection recurrence if left untreated after initial therapy completion stages are compromised even when conventional filling material performs well under ideal conditions yet struggles against biological constraints inherent during actual use scenarios involving human dentition variations across individuals needing customized care plans beyond one-size-fits-all solutions offered via rudimentary protocols lacking adaptability needed today given evolving scientific understanding around best practices necessary for optimal outcomes achievable only through integration between skilled clinicians utilizing state-of-the-art resources available currently enabling better overall dental healthcare delivery systems worldwide aiming towards zero tolerance policies toward suboptimal therapeutic interventions potentially harmful long term consequences otherwise likely resulting absent
